disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No bilious/faeculent vomiting and complete inability to pass any wind or motion
trigger_question_examples: Have you vomited, and if so what did it look like?
Have you been able to pass ANY wind or open your bowels at all this evening?
Is it complete inability to pass wind, or just difficult?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No vomiting, and I can still pass a little wind with effort — my bowels last opened this morning.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is colicky/waves (not constant/severe), no fever, no localised right lower quadrant pain
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain constant and severe, or does it come in waves?
Any fever, or pain settling into your lower right side?
Is the pain far worse than your tummy feels to press?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes in waves rather than constant, no fever, and it's not in the lower right — it's all over.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Onset after a large gluten (pasta) meal with a history of episodic gut symptoms and pending gluten-sensitivity serology
trigger_question_examples: Did this start after a particular meal?
Have you had similar bloating/cramps before, and are you being investigated for gluten sensitivity?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it started after a big pasta dinner, and I get milder versions of this often — my gluten-sensitivity test is pending.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I need a CT scan and hospital tonight for a blockage?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen obstruction/appendicitis/ischaemia red flags; if absent, explain a functional/gluten-sensitivity flare is most likely, offer antispasmodic and simple measures with a strict surgical-abdomen safety-net, and keep a low threshold for in-person review since the abdomen cannot be examined remotely — rather than defaulting to a midnight CT or dismissing outright.
